solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public String[] uncommonFromSentences(String s1, String s2) {
        java.util.Map<String, Integer> wordCount = new java.util.HashMap<>();
        for (String word : s1.split(" ")) {
            wordCount.put(word, wordCount.getOrDefault(word, 0) + 1);
        }
        for (String word : s2.split(" ")) {
            wordCount.put(word, wordCount.getOrDefault(word, 0) + 1);
        }

        java.util.List<String> result = new java.util.ArrayList<>();
        for (String word : wordCount.keySet()) {
            if (wordCount.get(word) == 1) {
                result.add(word);
            }
        }

        return result.toArray(new String[0]);
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(N + M)
space complexity: O(N + M)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a hashmap to count the frequency of each word in both sentences. It then collects all words that appear exactly once, which are the uncommon words. The approach is efficient and leverages hashmap operations for quick lookups and updates.
